task_completion.description: Camera is correctly positioned to face the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender 3D Viewport, the camera object is rotated to point towards the cube and ground plane, or the Camera View (Numpad 0) shows the cube clearly in frame.

task_completion.description: Sun light energy is restored to properly light the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Object Data Properties (light bulb icon) for the Sun light, the Strength/Energy is set to a value > 0.5, or the rendered viewport preview shows a brightly lit scene instead of darkness.

task_completion.description: BaseCube is set to be visible in renders
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Outliner panel (top right), the camera icon (Disable in Renders) next to 'BaseCube' is enabled/active, ensuring it will show up in the final render.

task_completion.description: Render resolution is set to at least 1280x720
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Properties panel (printer icon), the Resolution X is set to 1280 or higher, and Resolution Y is set to 720 or higher.

task_completion.description: Render samples are set to at least 16
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Render Properties panel (camera icon), the Render Samples (specifically for the Cycles engine) is set to 16 or higher.

task_completion.description: The corrected project file is saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application window title bar shows 'fixed_scene.blend', or a terminal/file browser confirms 'fixed_scene.blend' exists in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'.

task_completion.description: The final render is saved to the specified path and shows the correct output
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file browser, terminal, or Blender's Image Editor shows 'fixed_render.png' saved in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'. The image itself must be visible and depict a properly lit red cube on a ground plane.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with Blender to fix the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using the Blender GUI (navigating panels, changing properties, using the viewport) or Blender's internal Python console to diagnose and fix the issues.

integrity.description: The rendered image is genuinely produced from the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: The rendered image exactly matches the geometry and lighting of the Blender scene (a red cube on a plane) and is not a generic downloaded image or solid color block.

integrity.description: Agent properly saved the files rather than spoofing them
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using Blender's 'Save As' dialog for the .blend file and 'Image > Save As' in the Render Window for the .png file, rather than just renaming the original broken files via terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific bugs in a Blender scene: 1) Camera rotated away from the scene, 2) Sun light energy set to 0.0 (needs to be > 0.5), 3) Render resolution set to 10x10 (needs to be >= 1280x720), 4) Cycles render samples set to 1 (needs to be >= 16), and 5) BaseCube object has hide_render=True (needs to be visible). The final outputs must be saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/fixed_scene.blend and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/fixed_render.png.
